Composite: 52.3 (53.0 prior)
Manufacturing: 51.2 (52.4 prior)

tweets.text:
US 4Q GDP: +1.4% annualized vs +2.8% est.

Government spending fell at a -5.1% pace, the biggest drop since 2020. Federal spending saw its fastest decline since 1972.

Fiscal drag is real. Growth is slowing.

Watch bonds, watch the Fed, watch private demand.

tweets.author: @QuasarMarkets

tweets.text:
🌍 Geopolitics

🧭 Escalating US-Iran tensions increase demand for safe-haven assets, support gold and core bonds; caps risk appetite.

🧭 Christine Lagarde confirms she intends to complete her ECB term, reduces policy uncertainty, and stabilizes eurozone rate expectations.

🧭 Sourcing raw materials from Russia faces scrutiny following US sanctions, raises European energy supply risk; supports commodity volatility.

🧭 US reinforces military presence in the Middle East amid negotiations, adds geopolitical premium to oil and defense stocks.

🧭 Euro area private-sector activity expands at the fastest rate since November, improves EU growth sentiment; modestly supportive EUR.

💹 Economy & Markets

📊 India Infrastructure Output Rises for 3rd Month, construction strength supports steel and cement demand; energy output remains weak.

📊 Mexico Retail Sales Rise in December, sustained consumer momentum underpins MXN and domestic equities.

📊 Morocco's Consumer Deflation Deepens in January, increases room for accommodative policy; lowers local yield pressure.

📊 Latvia Producer Prices Rise in January, upstream price rebound signals mild inflation pickup.

📊 Serbia Producer Prices Slip in January, producer deflation reflects soft industrial demand.

📊 French 10-Year OAT Yield Falls to Six-Month Low, safe-haven flows compress eurozone yields.

📊 Italian 10-Year BTP Yield Falls to Four-Month Low, geopolitical hedging narrows spreads; supports periphery bonds.

📊 German Bund Yields Fall, risk-off tone reinforces core European bond demand.

📊 Croatia Jobless Rate Hits 10-Month High, labor softening pressures consumption outlook.

📊 Eurozone Negotiated Wage Growth Quickens in Q4, wage resilience complicates rapid ECB easing.

📊 UK Gilt Yields Drop as Record Budget Surplus Boosts Sentiment, strong fiscal balance compresses term premium.

📊 Montenegro Inflation Rate at 10-Month Low, inflation moderation improves macro stability.

📊 Slovenia Unemployment Rate at 10-Month High, labor cooling signals slower domestic demand.

📊 UK Services Sector Sustains Expansion in February, services resilience offsets manufacturing softness.

📊 UK Private Sector Activity Rises to 22-Month High, composite strength supports GBP and cyclicals.

📊 UK Manufacturing Growth Accelerates to 18-Month High, export rebound boosts industrial outlook.

📊 Poland Manufacturing Morale Hits 2021-High, sentiment improvement signals regional recovery traction.

📊 Greece Current Account Deficit Largest in Over 3 Years, widening external imbalance pressures sovereign outlook.

📊 Euro Area Services PMI Rises Less than Expected, Modest Services Growth Tempers Optimism.

📊 Euro Area Manufacturing PMI Tops Forecasts, Industrial Rebound Strengthens Cyclical Recovery Narrative.

⚔ Conflict & Security

⚠ US weighs measures to pressure Tehran during negotiations, sustained geopolitical uncertainty drives defensive positioning.

⚠ Middle East military buildup heightens global risk sensitivity, increases volatility in oil and FX markets.

⚠ Sanctions scrutiny intensifies over Russian energy sourcing, reinforces supply chain realignment across Europe.

🛢 Energy & Commodities

🛢 Crude Oil 66.163 (-0.36%), modest pullback reflects demand caution despite geopolitical tension.

🛢 Brent 71.388 (-0.38%), balanced market pricing; limited upside momentum.

🛢 Natural Gas 2.9684 (-0.92%), softer demand expectations weigh on pricing.

🛢 Gold 5022.76 (+0.50%), safe-haven bid on geopolitical risk.

🛢 Silver 80.377 (+3.53%), strong momentum signals hedging and industrial optimism.

🛢 Wheat 564.78 (+0.94%), supply concerns support the agricultural complex.

🛢 Lumber 568.00 (-2.49%), weaker housing demand pressures construction inputs.

🛢 Iron Ore CNY 746.00 (-2.10%), cooling Chinese demand weighs on bulk commodities.

🌦 Climate

🌱 Electricity output growth slows in India to 3.8%, moderating power demand signals softer industrial momentum.

🌱 Coal production growth eases in India to 3.1%, stable supply limits upside pressure.

💻 Technology

💻 Euro Area Manufacturing PMI signals the greatest improvement since June 2022, supports automation, industrial tech, and capital goods sectors.

💻 Export demand in UK manufacturing rises at fastest pace in four-and-a-half years, boosts advanced manufacturing and tech supply chains.

💻 Business sentiment in eurozone manufacturing reaches four-year high, strengthens capex and industrial investment outlook. 🌍 Geopolitics

🧭 Escalating US-Iran tensions increase demand for safe-haven assets, support gold and core bonds; caps risk appetite.

🧭 Christine Lagarde confirms she intends to complete her ECB term, reduces policy uncertainty, and stabilizes eurozone rate expectations.

🧭 Sourcing raw materials from Russia faces scrutiny following US sanctions, raises European energy supply risk; supports commodity volatility.

🧭 US reinforces military presence in the Middle East amid negotiations, adds geopolitical premium to oil and defense stocks.

🧭 Euro area private-sector activity expands at the fastest rate since November, improves EU growth sentiment; modestly supportive EUR.

💹 Economy & Markets

📊 India Infrastructure Output Rises for 3rd Month, construction strength supports steel and cement demand; energy output remains weak.

📊 Mexico Retail Sales Rise in December, sustained consumer momentum underpins MXN and domestic equities.

📊 Morocco's Consumer Deflation Deepens in January, increases room for accommodative policy; lowers local yield pressure.

📊 Latvia Producer Prices Rise in January, upstream price rebound signals mild inflation pickup.

📊 Serbia Producer Prices Slip in January, producer deflation reflects soft industrial demand.

📊 French 10-Year OAT Yield Falls to Six-Month Low, safe-haven flows compress eurozone yields.

📊 Italian 10-Year BTP Yield Falls to Four-Month Low, geopolitical hedging narrows spreads; supports periphery bonds.

📊 German Bund Yields Fall, risk-off tone reinforces core European bond demand.

📊 Croatia Jobless Rate Hits 10-Month High, labor softening pressures consumption outlook.

📊 Eurozone Negotiated Wage Growth Quickens in Q4, wage resilience complicates rapid ECB easing.

📊 UK Gilt Yields Drop as Record Budget Surplus Boosts Sentiment, strong fiscal balance compresses term premium.

📊 Montenegro Inflation Rate at 10-Month Low, inflation moderation improves macro stability.

📊 Slovenia Unemployment Rate at 10-Month High, labor cooling signals slower domestic demand.

📊 UK Services Sector Sustains Expansion in February, services resilience offsets manufacturing softness.

📊 UK Private Sector Activity Rises to 22-Month High, composite strength supports GBP and cyclicals.

📊 UK Manufacturing Growth Accelerates to 18-Month High, export rebound boosts industrial outlook.

📊 Poland Manufacturing Morale Hits 2021-High, sentiment improvement signals regional recovery traction.

📊 Greece Current Account Deficit Largest in Over 3 Years, widening external imbalance pressures sovereign outlook.

📊 Euro Area Services PMI Rises Less than Expected, Modest Services Growth Tempers Optimism.

📊 Euro Area Manufacturing PMI Tops Forecasts, Industrial Rebound Strengthens Cyclical Recovery Narrative.

⚔ Conflict & Security

⚠ US weighs measures to pressure Tehran during negotiations, sustained geopolitical uncertainty drives defensive positioning.

⚠ Middle East military buildup heightens global risk sensitivity, increases volatility in oil and FX markets.

⚠ Sanctions scrutiny intensifies over Russian energy sourcing, reinforces supply chain realignment across Europe.

🛢 Energy & Commodities

🛢 Crude Oil 66.163 (-0.36%), modest pullback reflects demand caution despite geopolitical tension.

🛢 Brent 71.388 (-0.38%), balanced market pricing; limited upside momentum.

🛢 Natural Gas 2.9684 (-0.92%), softer demand expectations weigh on pricing.

🛢 Gold 5022.76 (+0.50%), safe-haven bid on geopolitical risk.

🛢 Silver 80.377 (+3.53%), strong momentum signals hedging and industrial optimism.

🛢 Wheat 564.78 (+0.94%), supply concerns support the agricultural complex.

🛢 Lumber 568.00 (-2.49%), weaker housing demand pressures construction inputs.

🛢 Iron Ore CNY 746.00 (-2.10%), cooling Chinese demand weighs on bulk commodities.

🌦 Climate

🌱 Electricity output growth slows in India to 3.8%, moderating power demand signals softer industrial momentum.

🌱 Coal production growth eases in India to 3.1%, stable supply limits upside pressure.

💻 Technology

💻 Euro Area Manufacturing PMI signals the greatest improvement since June 2022, supports automation, industrial tech, and capital goods sectors.

💻 Export demand in UK manufacturing rises at fastest pace in four-and-a-half years, boosts advanced manufacturing and tech supply chains.

💻 Business sentiment in eurozone manufacturing reaches four-year high, strengthens capex and industrial investment outlook.

tweets.text:
AI agents can think.
But they can't reliably share their skills.

Agents are stuck reinventing the same capabilities.
No universal system for discovering skills.
No persistent infrastructure for machine capability.

That's a massive bottleneck for the agentic internet. ↓ AI agents can think.

tweets.text:
El CEO de ClickUp acaba de dejarlo claro: el futuro del software no es añadir funciones infinitas, es la CONVERGENCIA a través de la IA. El CEO de ClickUp acaba de dejarlo claro: el futuro del software no es añadir funciones infinitas, es la CONVERGENCIA a través de la IA.


Zeb Evans (@DJ_CURFEW)

"I will learn to code and I will create a BILLION dollar idea with this laptop."
I wrote that in 2013.
I bet EVERYTHING on that laptop.

I built a business that generated almost $3m cash. Then I shut it down overnight.

And I bet it ALL on ClickUp. Every cent.
I bet on our team.
I bet on moving to Silicon Valley.
A house we could barely afford.
5am standups. Building something nobody else could see.

Every bet got BIGGER.

Today, some say it paid off.
1,300+ employees. 3,200+ agents. Multi-billion-dollar valuation.

But I'm not done.

My message to our company: GO ALL IN.
I'm betting the entire company on what I see as the future of software and AI - CONVERGENCE.

But that's how it's always felt. Right before the bet hits.

If you're sitting there with your own laptop, your own crazy idea, and everyone thinks you're nuts...

Bet on it.

What was your bet?




— https://nitter.net/DJ_CURFEW/status/2024532853346730352#m

Google acaba de lanzar una IA que reemplaza sesiones de fotos completas con una sola imagen.

Estudios. Fotógrafos. Retocadores. Equipos de marketing.

Miles de empleos borrados silenciosamente. Google acaba de lanzar una IA que reemplaza sesiones de fotos completas con una sola imagen.

Estudios. Fotógrafos. Retocadores. Equipos de marketing.

Miles de empleos borrados silenciosamente.


Google Labs (@GoogleLabs)

Today, we’re introducing Pomelli’s latest feature update, ‘Photoshoot’ 

With Photoshoot, you can start from a single image of your product and easily create high quality, customized product shots to elevate your marketing. 

Available free of charge in the US, Canada, Australia & New Zealand! Get started with Pomelli today at labs.google/pomelli

Video
  




— https://nitter.net/GoogleLabs/status/2024529795548102667#m

La ciencia de la viralidad, ahora automatizada.

🔗 Comienza tu camino viral: buzzy.now/

Kimi K2.5 running across both Mac Studios using @exolabs Also will have MiniMax 2.5 running at the same time. Opus 4.6 will power Henry, the main orchestrator. The local MiniMax will power the agent on the Mac Mini. Kimi K2.5 will power the 2nd Studio Notes on my planned setup:

tweets.text:
R to @AlexFinn: What you're looking at are 2 Mac Studios with 512gb of memory each and a base Mac Mini.

The point of this post isn't to say you have to buy thousands of dollars of equipment or you're cooked. It's not that AT ALL

All I'm saying is prepare for a future when you're intelligence is local. Where you have an agent working for you on your desk 24/7

The compute required for this future will only get cheaper over time. You don't need a $10,000 Mac Studio

I'm simply diving all the way into the deep end because nobody has swam in this part of the pool before. I want to see what's over here. I want to see what's possible.

I'm taking you along for the ride. What you're looking at are 2 Mac Studios with 512gb of memory each and a base Mac Mini.

The point of this post isn't to say you have to buy thousands of dollars of equipment or you're cooked. It's not that AT ALL

I'm taking you along for the ride.
